threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the road at pedestrian lights instead of just weaving through traffic like a local. Those buttons are just for show.
Caught a late 59 last weekend; a bloke in a top hat serenaded the driver with a sax. You never know what you'll hear at 2 am in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy in a suit bartering with a street vendor over a kebab while his dog wore a tiny bowtie. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a techno rave in an abandoned factory on a Tuesday night—where the dress code is sheer creativity and the after-party is at the Kiezkneipe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their disdain for tourists who still think Dolores Park is just for picnics. It's a social battlefield now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus with a full-on opera performance in the back. Only in SF will you find a stage on wheels. Classic Muni moment!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume every corner café serves churros. Most locals prefer a simple pa amb tomàquet and a café amb llet, no frills.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their reluctance to sit at a bar table when there are empty seats; they still expect to wait for a 'proper' table.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the Overground' when waiting for the Dalston Junction train. Proper locals know it's all in the branding.
Heard a guy on the 333 last night trying to serenade the driver with his own version of 'Wonderwall'—he's lucky it didn't turn into a duet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: As I walked past the tempelhof park, I smelled freshly roasted coffee mixed with the unmistakable scent of street food; that’s when I knew I belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a spontaneous concert break out in a U-Bahn station during a strike, with people dancing on the platforms. It’s pure magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they’ll walk past a barking dog on the train tracks but stand frozen at the coffee shop for 15 minutes deciding on a pour-over.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 9 at Civic Center, stopped for a dog on the tracks. We sat in awkward silence, everyone smiling at the pup instead of each other.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all sparrow songs are the same, but if you sit in Vallcarca, you'll hear them mimicking the church bells—a true local serenade.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled look at the bus schedule. Locals know it's a guideline, not a timetable.
